context.title: SAP's Acquisition of Reltio to Integrate External Data into its AI Platform
context.core_question: How can SAP successfully integrate Reltio's master data management capabilities to pull external, non-SAP data into its AI platform and Business Data Cloud, thereby increasing adoption and delivering on the promise of business AI?
context.current_state: SAP has announced the acquisition of master data management specialist Reltio. This follows the 2025 launch of SAP's Business Data Cloud (BDC), developed with Databricks to create AI-powered insight apps. However, SAP is struggling with BDC adoption—user group research shows 83% of members have low familiarity with it, and there are reports of customers stuck in mid-migration paying full fees for an unusable system. The acquisition signals a strategic shift from BDC's original focus on sharing SAP data externally to now harmonizing external data for use within SAP's AI ecosystem.
context.core_dilemma: SAP needs to integrate external data to make its AI platform valuable, but it must overcome low market awareness of BDC, migration challenges that are damaging its reputation, and a fundamental shift in BDC's strategic purpose—from exposing SAP data outward to pulling external data inward.
context.key_decision_point: How to execute the post-acquisition integration of Reltio's technology and customer base to rapidly demonstrate value, fix the migration/reputation issues with BDC, and clearly communicate the new, combined value proposition of unified internal and external data for AI.
target_outcomes: Increased adoption and familiarity with SAP's Business Data Cloud (BDC)
Successful integration of non-SAP data into SAP's AI platform via Reltio's systems
Delivery of 'business AI' that leverages unified, contextual data from both SAP and external sources
Resolution of the mid-migration customer issues and associated reputational damage
hard_constraints: Must integrate Reltio's AI-based entity resolution system with SAP's existing application portfolio (ERP, HR, CRM, etc.)
Must address the existing BDC customer base experiencing migration problems
Must work within the technical architecture of Reltio's system, which migrated to Spanner on Google Cloud in 2024
known_unknowns: Whether the acquisition will successfully boost adoption of SAP's Business Data Cloud
How effectively Reltio's system will integrate data across SAP and non-SAP applications for AI agent development
The full financial terms and integration costs of the acquisition

signals.signal: SAP announces acquisition of Reltio, a master data management firm, to pull non-SAP data into its AI platform and Business Data Cloud (BDC).
User group research (DSAG) shows 83% of members have low or no familiarity with BDC, indicating a major awareness/adoption problem.
BDC customers stuck in mid-migration are paying full cloud fees for an unusable system, a finding predicted to 'haunt SAP's sales deck.'
Analyst notes the acquisition represents a strategic shift for BDC: from sharing SAP data externally to harmonizing external data for internal SAP AI use.
Reltio uses an AI-based entity resolution system to create 'golden records' and migrated its database to Spanner on Google Cloud in 2024.

assumptions_explicit.target_unknown: Whether the acquisition will successfully boost adoption of SAP's Business Data Cloud
assumptions_explicit.assumption_text: Integrating Reltio's external data capabilities will make BDC more valuable and thus increase adoption, provided migration issues are resolved.

assumptions_explicit.target_unknown: How effectively Reltio's system will integrate data across SAP and non-SAP applications for AI agent development
assumptions_explicit.assumption_text: Reltio's AI-based entity resolution and 'golden record' system is technically capable of integrating across SAP and non-SAP domains, though implementation complexity is high.

assumptions_explicit.target_unknown: The full financial terms and integration costs of the acquisition
assumptions_explicit.assumption_text: The acquisition and integration costs are significant but justified by SAP's strategic need to unify data for its AI platform.
The reputational damage from the BDC migration failures is a critical near-term blocker that must be addressed before the new value proposition can gain traction.
The shift in BDC's strategic purpose (outward to inward) will cause confusion in the market and require clear re-communication.

high_leverage_unknowns: Whether the acquisition will successfully boost adoption of SAP's Business Data Cloud
How effectively Reltio's system will integrate data across SAP and non-SAP applications for AI agent development
The full financial terms and integration costs of the acquisition
